en: What then shall we say was gained by Abraham, our forefather according to the flesh?
zh: 那麼，論到在血統 上作我們祖先的亞伯拉罕所經驗的，我們可以說甚麼呢？

en: For if Abraham was justified by works, he has something to boast about, but not before God.
zh: 亞伯拉罕若是因行為稱義，就有可誇的，只是不能在 神面前誇口。

en: For what does the Scripture say? "Abraham believed God, and it was counted to him as righteousness."
zh: 經上怎麼樣說呢？“亞伯拉罕信 神，這就算為他的義。”

en: Now to the one who works, his wages are not counted as a gift but as his due.
zh: 作工的得工資，不算是恩典，是他應得的。

en: And to the one who does not work but trusts him who justifies the ungodly, his faith is counted as righteousness,
zh: 可是，那不作工而只信那稱不敬虔的人為義的 神的，他的信就算為義了。

en: just as David also speaks of the blessing of the one to whom God counts righteousness apart from works:
zh: 大衛也是這樣說，那不靠行為而蒙 神算為義的人是有福的！

en: "Blessed are those whose lawless deeds are forgiven, and whose sins are covered;
zh: “過犯得蒙赦免，罪惡得到遮蓋的人，是有福的；

en: blessed is the man against whom the Lord will not count his sin."
zh: 主不算為有罪的，這人是有福的。”

en: Is this blessing then only for the circumcised, or also for the uncircumcised? We say that faith was counted to Abraham as righteousness.
zh: 這樣看來，那有福的人，是指受割禮的呢，還是也指沒有受割禮的呢？因為我們說：“亞伯拉罕的信算為他的義。”

en: How then was it counted to him? Was it before or after he had been circumcised? It was not after, but before he was circumcised.
zh: 那麼，是怎樣算的呢？是在他受割禮以後呢，還是在他受割禮之前呢？不是在他受割禮以後，而是在他受了割禮以前。

en: He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that he had by faith while he was still uncircumcised. The purpose was to make him the father of all who believe without being circumcised, so that righteousness would be counted to them as well,
zh: 他領受了割禮為記號，作他受割禮之前因信稱義的印證，使他作所有沒有受割禮而信之人的父，使他們也被算為義；

en: and to make him the father of the circumcised who are not merely circumcised but who also walk in the footsteps of the faith that our father Abraham had before he was circumcised.
zh: 又作受割禮之人的父，就是作那些不僅受割禮，並且照著我們祖宗亞伯拉罕未受割禮時就信的那腳蹤而行之人的父。

en: For the promise to Abraham and his offspring that he would be heir of the world did not come through the law but through the righteousness of faith.
zh: 原來 神給亞伯拉罕和他後裔承受世界的應許，並不是因著律法，而是藉著因信而來的義。

en: For if it is the adherents of the law who are to be the heirs, faith is null and the promise is void.
zh: 如果屬於律法的人才能成為後嗣，信就沒有作用，應許也就落空了。

en: For the law brings wrath, but where there is no law there is no transgression.
zh: 因為律法帶來刑罰，沒有律法，就沒有違背律法的事。

en: That is why it depends on faith, in order that the promise may rest on grace and be guaranteed to all his offspring- not only to the adherent of the law but also to the one who shares the faith of Abraham, who is the father of us all,
zh: 所以，成為後嗣是因著信，為的是要按著 神的恩典，使給所有後裔的應許堅定不移，不但臨到那屬於律法的人，也臨到那效法亞伯拉罕而信的人。亞伯拉罕在 神面前作我們眾人的父，

en: as it is written, "I have made you the father of many nations"- in the presence of the God in whom he believed, who gives life to the dead and calls into existence the things that do not exist.
zh: 如經上所記：“我已經立了你作萬國的父。”他所信的，是叫死人得生命、使無變為有的 神。

en: In hope he believed against hope, that he should become the father of many nations, as he had been told, "So shall your offspring be."
zh: 在他沒有盼望的時候，仍然懷著盼望去信，因此就成了萬國的父，正如 神所說：“你的後裔將要這樣眾多。”

en: He did not weaken in faith when he considered his own body, which was as good as dead (since he was about a hundred years old), or when he considered the barrenness of Sarah's womb.
zh: 他快到一百歲的時候，想到自己的身體好像已經死了，撒拉也不能生育，他的信心還是不軟弱，

en: No distrust made him waver concerning the promise of God, but he grew strong in his faith as he gave glory to God,
zh: 也沒有因著不信而疑惑 神的應許，反倒堅信不移，把榮耀歸給 神，

en: fully convinced that God was able to do what he had promised.
zh: 滿心相信 神所應許的， 神必能成就。

en: That is why his faith was "counted to him as righteousness."
zh: 因此，這就算為他的義。

en: But the words "it was counted to him" were not written for his sake alone,
zh: “算為他的義”這一句話，不是單為他寫的，

en: but for ours also. It will be counted to us who believe in him who raised from the dead Jesus our Lord,
zh: 也是為我們這將來得算為義的人寫的，就是為我們這信 神使我們的主耶穌從死人中復活的人寫的。

en: who was delivered up for our trespasses and raised for our justification.
zh: 耶穌為我們的過犯被交去處死，為我們的稱義而復活。
